description_long: This object is a longboard-style hoverboard with a natural wood-colored deck and four large yellow wheels, designed for personal transportation in urban environments. The deck is elongated and slightly concave, with cutouts above the wheels to prevent wheel bite and to allow for sharper turns. The surface of the deck appears smooth with a visible logo and some printed text, suggesting branding and possible grip enhancement. The trucks and wheel mounts are metallic, painted in a contrasting color, and the wheels are thick, likely made of polyurethane for shock absorption and grip. The overall construction suggests a blend of durability and flexibility, suitable for both cruising and maneuvering on city streets. The design is streamlined, with no visible lighting or electronic controls, emphasizing a classic, manual riding experience.
description: A longboard-style hoverboard with a natural wood deck, large yellow wheels, and a smooth, streamlined design featuring visible branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin, slightly concave wooden deck and the large yellow wheels extending below.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full deck shape, visible logo, and the placement of the wheels at both ends.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the deck's profile and wheel arrangement.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view displaying the underside of the deck, wheel mounts, and the trucks' structure.
attribute_wheel_configuration: Four large, parallel wheels, two at each end of the deck, mounted on metal trucks; wheels are wide and made of yellow polyurethane.
attribute_footpad_design: The footpad area is the entire upper surface of the deck, elongated and slightly concave, made of smooth wood with a visible logo; no raised edges or grip tape, but the surface may have a light texture for traction.
attribute_brand_logo: A brand logo and some text are visible on the top surface of the deck, centrally located and printed in dark ink.
